input.prompt:
I have two CLR activation logs for the same tool (mt.exe). When I run
the build from my script, it works fine. When I run MSBuild directly
from cmd.exe, I get FOD dialogs. Both logs show the same DllGetClassObject
activation for the same CLSID. Why is the behavior different?

data.content:

the build from my script, it works fine. When I run MSBuild directly

activation for the same CLSID. Why is the behavior different?

the build from my script, it works fine. When I run MSBuild directly

data.content:
The key difference is on **line 20** of each log — the `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS` flag:

| Log | Value | Behavior (line 37) |
|---|---|---|
| **cmdline** | `0` | `Launching feature-on-demand installation…` |
| **script** | `32769` (0x8001) | `Could have launched…if was not opted out` |

Your script's process has `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS` (0x0001) and `SEM_NOOPENFILEERRORBOX` (0x8000) set via `SetErrorMode`. The CLR shim checks this flag before launching `fondue.exe` to install .NET Framework 3.5 — when the flag is set, it **suppresses** the FOD dialog and silently fails instead.

**The root cause:** mt.exe requires .NET Framework 2.0/3.5 (v2.0.50727), which isn't installed. Both environments hit the same error (`Unable to find a version of the runtime to use`). The only difference is whether the process error mode allows the UI prompt.

**To fix:** Either install .NET Framework 3.5 (`DISM /Online /Enable-Feature /FeatureName:NetFx3`), or set `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS` in your cmd.exe environment too if you want to suppress the dialog there as well.
data.reasoningText:
Now I can see the key difference between the two logs. Let me compare them carefully:

Both logs show the same CLSID activation, the same error ("Unable to find a version of the runtime to use"), and the same resolution to v2.0.50727 (.NET Framework 3.x). The critical difference is:

**From cmdline (line 20):** `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS is set to 0`
**From script (line 20):** `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS is set to 32769`

When `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS` is 0 (not set), the CLR shim proceeds to launch the Feature-on-Demand (FOD) installation dialog:


- cmdline line 37: `Launching feature-on-demand installation. CmdLine: "C:\WINDOWS\system32\fondue.exe" /enable-feature:NetFx3 /caller-name:mscoreei.dll`

When `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS` is set (32769 = 0x8001 = SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS | SEM_NOOPENFILEERRORBOX), the CLR shim skips the FOD dialog:
- script line 37: `Could have launched feature-on-demand installation if was not opted out. CmdLine: ...`

So the error handling mode is what determines whether the system attempts to automatically install the missing .NET Framework or just fails silently. which suppresses the FOD dialog from appearing.
